experiences.intentions: The board is empty except for the active S piece near the top, currently horizontal. With an empty board and next pieces O, L, Z, J, the main objective is to place this piece cleanly to preserve flexibility and avoid awkward overhangs. Candidate actions: moving left or right starts positioning but does not finish the placement; rotating would make a vertical S, which is usually less convenient on an empty board; soft_drop advances only one row. Since there are no threats and the current horizontal S can be safely placed on the flat floor, the best immediate progress is to hard drop it and lock in a simple low stack.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Board is empty except the active O at the top center and a settled S near the bottom center. O has no meaningful rotation, so rotate actions do nothing useful. Goal is to place pieces cleanly and build a flat stack without creating holes; placing O near the existing S can help level the surface. From the spawn column, moving left starts aligning the O toward the left side of the S structure, while hard/soft drop immediately would place it high above center and reduce placement flexibility. So the best immediate step is to shift left.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The active O piece is near the top center and cannot benefit from rotation. The stack is very low, with only an S-piece formation at the bottom center creating a shallow notch. My goal is to place the O efficiently to build a flat, compact surface without creating holes. Candidate actions: rotate_left/right do nothing useful for O; no_op delays; left/right could reposition for a later better placement, but with only one move now and no immediate threat, the strongest progress is to commit the piece downward. hard_drop quickly places the O on the left side of the bottom S structure, filling the area around columns 3-4 above the existing blocks and keeping the stack compact for upcoming L/Z/J/T pieces.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active L is near the top in spawn area; stack is very low with only O and S/Z structure at the bottom center-left, so there is no immediate threat. The goal now is efficient placement to keep the surface flat and preserve flexibility for upcoming Z, J, T, I. A quick hard drop in the current orientation would likely place the L on top of the small bottom stack around columns 4-6, creating extra height and bumps. Moving horizontally first is better; shifting left aims to tuck the L toward the left side where it can complement the O block and leave cleaner central space. Among candidates, left improves future placement options more than right or dropping immediately, while rotation is premature before aligning over the target zone.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The active L piece is high above a mostly empty board, with the only stack at the bottom forming a low O/S structure around columns 3-6. There is no immediate threat, so the goal is to start moving the L toward a useful placement rather than dropping it prematurely in the center. A good sub-goal is to shift it toward the left so it can later tuck against the existing stack or build a flatter surface while preserving flexibility for future Z, J, T, I pieces. Among actions, left improves horizontal alignment toward that target; right moves away, rotations are not yet necessary with so much vertical space, and dropping now reduces flexibility.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The active L is high above a mostly flat stack with a shallow structure near the bottom: O occupies columns 3-4 on rows 17-18, S occupies columns 5-6/4-5 on rows 18-19, leaving open space on the left and a notch developing around columns 2-3. Goal is to place the L efficiently without creating overhangs, ideally using the left side and preserving flexibility for upcoming Z,J,T,I. Candidate actions: hard_drop now would place the current horizontal L high impact but without lateral adjustment; rotation is not yet necessary because horizontal orientation can fit well. Moving left shifts the L toward the open left side, setting up a cleaner drop into columns 0-2/1-3 on subsequent turns. Right would crowd the center over the existing O/S, and dropping immediately from current x is less controlled. Best sub-goal now is to start aligning left before descent.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active L piece is high above the stack; the only occupied cells are a low O/S structure near the center-right. Immediate threat is low, so the goal is efficient placement for future line clears and preserving a flat surface. Current L in spawn orientation would likely be best moved toward the left wall and dropped later, since the center is already partly occupied and upcoming Z,J,T,I benefit from cleaner middle access. Among single-step actions, moving left starts positioning toward the open left side without committing to a poor drop or unnecessary rotation.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The active piece is an L high on the left side, while the stack is very low with only O and S near the bottom center-right. There is no immediate threat, so the goal is to place the L efficiently to keep the surface flat and set up future Z,J,T,I placements. Dropping it now in its current orientation would likely create an awkward left-side overhang and isolate space. Moving right starts shifting the piece toward the central stack where it can be rotated and fit more cleanly on later turns. Other actions are less useful now: left pushes it into the wall, rotations are premature before horizontal alignment, and dropping immediately wastes placement flexibility.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The stack is very low and centered-right: O at columns 3-4 on row 17, O/S/S across rows 18-19 creating a shallow surface with a 1-cell step. The active L is high up with plenty of freedom, so there is no immediate threat. Goal is to place the L to keep the surface flat and avoid covering awkward gaps before upcoming Z, J, T, I. A good sub-goal is to move the L toward the right side where it can later fit around the existing bump near the S/O structure; dropping immediately in the current left-central position would create unnecessary height and asymmetry. Among candidates, right starts shifting toward the more useful placement zone while preserving all future rotation/drop options; left moves away from the current stack, rotations are premature while still far above the board, and dropping now commits too early.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active L is high above a low central stack with O and S already forming a small mound around columns 3-6. Goal is to place L to keep the surface flat and avoid creating holes while setting up upcoming Z/J/T/I. Immediate lateral/rotation planning matters more than dropping now. Rotating right to vertical orientation lets the L fit more cleanly beside the central stack on future moves than staying flat; left/right alone without rotation is less flexible, and hard/soft drop now would commit too early from a high position. So best action is to rotate toward the useful vertical placement.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Board is mostly empty with a small stack at the center-bottom: O at columns 3-4 above mixed O/S/SS shape, creating a gentle right slope and open flat space around it. Active L is high and vertical (rotation 1). Goal now is to place the L to build a flatter low stack and avoid creating holes. Candidate actions: hard_drop in current orientation would likely land the vertical L near the existing center stack, probably leaving an awkward tall notch. Moving left gives access to flatter empty columns and can set up a horizontal L placement later. Moving right risks stacking on the existing slope. Rotating now is possible, but first shifting left preserves options and heads toward a cleaner placement area. Best immediate sub-goal is to reposition left before dropping.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an L in a vertical-right-foot orientation, currently high above a low uneven stack near the center-bottom. The surface has O at columns 3-4 and S/Z shape around columns 4-6, leaving relatively open space on the left and right. Goal is to place the L to build a flatter foundation without creating holes, ideally preparing for upcoming Z/J/T/I. Immediate sub-goal is to move the piece toward a useful landing zone before committing. Candidate actions: hard_drop would lock too early near current column and may not be optimal; soft_drop just reduces time without improving alignment; rotations can be done later if needed. Moving left shifts the L toward the open left side where it can likely stand cleanly against the existing stack and preserve central flexibility. Moving right heads toward the S stack and risks awkward overhangs. Best action now is left.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Current active piece is an L in vertical orientation near the left side, with an existing O/S stack low in the center-right. The board is mostly empty, so the main opportunity is to place this L cleanly to build a flat foundation and avoid creating overhangs. Dropping it in the current column would make a small left bump but is still safe; moving or rotating first could improve placement later, but with only one action now, the strongest progress is to advance the piece downward quickly while keeping future flexibility. hard_drop secures the piece immediately onto the floor at the far left, creating a simple low stack without threatening holes, and sets up the upcoming Z/J/T/I pieces on a mostly open board.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active J is high near the left-center with a mostly flat open field above a small stack at the bottom. experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The active J is high near the left-center with plenty of horizontal freedom, and the stack below is low with a left-side L column, center O/S structure, and no immediate danger. experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active J is high on the left in horizontal orientation. The stack is low and centered/right, with the left side relatively open above an L column. Goal is to place J efficiently without creating overhangs, ideally moving it toward the left wall so it can later drop into the left-side contour; there is no immediate threat since the board is very low. Candidate actions: hard_drop would freeze it far too early near the top, bad. Soft_drop commits downward before aligning. Rotations are not yet necessary because current horizontal J can still be shifted, and vertical forms are less immediately useful. Right moves away from the useful left-side well. So the best immediate sub-goal is to shift left toward the wall.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active J is high on the far left in horizontal orientation with a mostly flat/low stack concentrated at the bottom center-right; left wall is open and there is no immediate threat. experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active T is near the top over a mostly flat left-middle stack with a central notch structure lower down. Immediate threats are low because the board is only partially filled, so the goal is efficient placement toward future I/Z/I/J pieces while avoiding unnecessary holes. From spawn, moving horizontally before locking is best; hard/soft drop now would place the T high and waste flexibility, and rotating now is not clearly beneficial because the current flat-up T can still be shifted into a better column next turn. Shifting left starts positioning the T toward the left-center surface where it can later slot more cleanly than staying centered/right.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active T is high near center with a mostly open field above a lumpy stack. experiences.intentions: The active I piece is vertical in column 4 near the top. The surface below has a deep open left-center well around columns 2-4, while the right side is bumpier with T/S/Z blocks. My goal is to place the I piece to improve board flatness and avoid creating holes. Rotating to horizontal now would let it eventually span the open trench, but with only one action this turn the best immediate step is to rotate it first, since moving left/right while still vertical does not yet prepare the strongest placement. Hard dropping vertically here would leave a tall pillar and waste the I. So rotate clockwise to horizontal.

Immediate threats are low since the stack is far from the top. Hard dropping the current horizontal I into the open area can help level the surface quickly and safely while keeping the board manageable.

Rotating left or right both reach the flat orientation from the current vertical state, and rotate_right is explicitly shown valid, so I’ll orient the T horizontally first.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a Z at the upper-left/mid-left, currently vertical (rotation 1), sitting above the left side where there is a horizontal I on row 14 and uneven stacks below. Immediate goal is to place the Z to improve the left surface without creating a deep overhang. Candidate moves: left would push the vertical Z farther against the wall and likely worsen stacking; right can set up a better placement over the center-left notch; rotate to horizontal could fit differently but there is no urgency to rotate before shifting, and hard/soft drop now would commit to a mediocre placement. Best sub-goal is to move toward the center-left gap first.

Surface has a 4-wide horizontal gap on row 13 at columns 2-5 that can be perfectly filled by dropping the I in its current horizontal orientation. Hard drop accomplishes this fastest and safely.

experiences.intentions: The active J piece is high at the top with plenty of time before lock. The stack below is uneven but not near the top; the main opportunity is to place this J to support flatter surfaces without committing prematurely. Immediate hard/soft drop from the current column would land on the row-10 I bar and create awkward overhangs. Rotations at the spawn area are all valid, so the best sub-goal now is to start aligning the J toward a more useful placement on the left side, where existing L/J structure can accept it better. Moving left preserves flexibility for future rotations and drops, while right would steer it toward a bumpier center-right with Z/T clutter. Therefore the best current action is to shift left.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is J near the top in horizontal orientation. The stack below is low but somewhat uneven, with a left-side cavity structure around rows 15-19 and relatively open center/top. Immediate threat is minimal since piece is near spawn and board is far from topping out. Goal now is to place the J efficiently to improve the lower-left terrain or avoid creating holes; since only one action is allowed this turn, the best sub-goal is to start moving it toward a useful landing zone before dropping. Candidate actions: hard_drop would commit early near the spawn columns and likely waste the J high above useful structure; soft_drop also commits downward without alignment. Rotations don’t seem immediately necessary because current horizontal J can be shifted first and adjusted later. Moving left begins steering toward the more promising left-side contour and preserves flexibility for future rotations/drops. Therefore choose left.

experiences.intentions: The active J is high on the board with no immediate danger from the stack below. The surface has a central valley and some unevenness, so the current goal is to start moving the J toward a useful tuck/stacking position rather than dropping it prematurely from the spawn area. Rotating now is unnecessary because the current horizontal J can still be adjusted later, while shifting left helps align it over the left side where there is existing structure and preserves future placement options. Candidate actions: hard_drop/soft_drop commit too early from a very high position; right moves away from the left structure; rotations don't yet create a clear advantage. Best action: move left.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a J near the top-left in rotation 0. The stack below has moderate height with a left-side structure and relatively open upper board, so there is no immediate top-out threat. Goal is to place this J to improve the left side and avoid creating awkward overhangs while setting up future S,O,L,T pieces. Candidate moves: hard_drop now would lock the current horizontal J high on the left without adjustment; rotations could make a vertical J useful, but first shifting left is stronger because the piece is already near the left wall and can likely slot more cleanly into the left contour on subsequent turns. Soft drop is premature before horizontal alignment. Best immediate sub-goal is to move the piece left toward the wall for a cleaner eventual placement.

experiences.intentions: Current active piece is a J near the upper-left, already resting just above/into the stack. The lower board has uneven terrain with some cavities, but the immediate priority is to place this J cleanly without creating a worse overhang. Candidate rotations mostly just shift the J shape around the same left area; moving right would require multiple turns and is not possible in one action. Hard dropping now would lock the current horizontal J across the far left, which leaves a protrusion and is less flexible. Rotating right to orientation 1 makes the J vertical with its foot to the right, a more compact shape on the left edge and sets up a cleaner eventual placement into the left-side contour. Soft drop/no_op are less purposeful than improving orientation now. Best action: rotate_right.

experiences.intentions: The active piece is a J near the upper-left, currently vertical with its foot to the right. The stack is low and uneven but not threatening yet; there is broad open space above, so the priority is efficient placement rather than emergency survival. A useful sub-goal is to settle this J onto the left side to fill the notch over the existing left structure and keep the center open for upcoming S,O,L,T. Immediate lateral or rotation moves are possible, but since the board below the current column is clear for many rows, advancing downward now preserves flexibility while moving toward placement. Hard drop would commit too early high on the left and likely create awkward overhangs; left/right or rotation are premature before getting closer to the surface. Soft drop is the best incremental move.

The stack below is uneven, with a clear vertical well/opening on the far right side and some clutter in the center-left. My goal is to place the I where it can safely descend and ideally preserve flexibility for upcoming S,O,L,T pieces. Moving right shifts the I toward the open right side, setting up a possible clean vertical drop path or better landing than staying over the rougher center. Left would move toward taller obstructing blocks, and dropping now would commit to a mediocre center placement. So the best immediate action is to move right.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is I, currently horizontal near the top-center resting on support. Surface below has a useful vertical well around column 2-3 on the left side; rotating to vertical now is valid and positions the I to potentially drop into that channel for better future line clears. Immediate hard/soft drop horizontally would create a flat but less valuable placement. Moving first is possible, but the key sub-goal is to orient the I vertically while it is safely supported. Among actions, rotate_right achieves the valid vertical orientation shown as Rotation 3 and preserves options next turn to shift/drop into the well.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is S near the top in horizontal orientation. Stack is low-to-mid with no immediate top-out threat, and the surface has some unevenness around the right-center/lower-right. Goal is to place this S efficiently to avoid creating holes and set up for upcoming O/L/T pieces. From the spawn position, moving right would likely better align the S over the right-side contours than staying or moving left; rotating now makes it vertical but there is no urgent need yet, and dropping immediately from here would strand it high over flatter empty space without committing to a better column. So the best incremental action is to shift right toward a more useful landing zone.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is S near the top with room to maneuver; the stack is low-mid and uneven, with some cavities in the lower left/center but no immediate danger. Goal is to place the S efficiently to avoid creating overhangs and keep the surface smooth for upcoming O/L/T. Candidate actions: hard_drop now would commit to the current horizontal S placement high on the left-center, likely premature; soft_drop also commits downward before aligning. left/right can improve horizontal placement later, but the immediate tactical opportunity is rotation: the vertical S orientation can fit more flexibly into notches and is easier to steer while descending. Since there is no threat and rotation is valid, rotate now to preserve options and target a cleaner tuck.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an S near the top with two visible orientations. The stack is moderate with some unevenness and a right-side cavity around the T/Z area, but dropping immediately from the spawn area won’t place it well yet. Goal is to position the S to improve fit and avoid creating overhangs while preserving future placements for upcoming O/L/T. Among actions, rotate_right would only change to horizontal S at the same high position without immediate benefit; drops are premature; left moves away from the likely right-center fit. Moving right advances the piece toward the open right-side lanes where S is more likely to slot cleanly on subsequent turns.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is S near the top-right, currently horizontal over columns roughly 6-8 with open space below. Surface below has existing stack concentrated left/center and some structure on right; there is no immediate threat at the top. Goal is to place the S efficiently to improve the right-side contour and avoid leaving awkward overhangs. Candidate actions: hard_drop would commit immediately in the current spawn column, likely onto the high right stack but without checking final fit after horizontal movement; left/right can reposition but with current information a useful immediate improvement is rotating to vertical to potentially slot into the right-side notch and preserve flexibility for subsequent movement/drops. soft_drop commits downward too early; no_op wastes tempo. Best sub-goal now is orient the S vertically before moving/dropping.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

Best sub-goal now is to descend the piece along the open right side and decide final placement after more information from lower rows, so soft_drop is best.

experiences.intentions: Active L is high near center-left in spawn area with no immediate collision threat. Surface below is uneven, and we likely want to place this L to improve left-center structure rather than commit instantly with a hard drop. Among actions, rotating right changes from flat spawn L to a vertical L that is more flexible for fitting into columns; left/right only shifts without improving shape yet, and dropping now would commit before alignment. Sub-goal is to set up a useful orientation first.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The active L piece is high near the center-right in vertical orientation. The stack below is uneven but not threatening yet; the immediate opportunity is to place this piece efficiently without creating overhangs. The lower-left and center have existing structures, while the far right has some jagged surface around columns 7-9. A good sub-goal is to move the L toward a cleaner placement zone and preserve flexibility for upcoming T,L,J. From the current spawn, rotating is not urgently better than first shifting horizontally; dropping now would commit too early. Moving right starts steering the piece toward the more open right side where an L can later tuck or stand without worsening the center.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is L in rotation 1 near the top, with a relatively uneven lower stack and no immediate danger at the spawn area. The best short-term goal is to place the L efficiently without creating extra overhangs, and likely toward the left side where there is a vertical well/step structure around the J and L pieces that an L can fit into later. Among immediate actions, moving left starts positioning the piece toward more useful columns, while rotating now is not clearly necessary and dropping immediately would commit to a mediocre placement high on the right. So the best action this turn is to shift left.

experiences.intentions: Active L is high and unobstructed. The stack has a central-right valley and no immediate top-out threat, so the goal is to place this L cleanly to improve surface shape without creating overhangs. Candidate moves: hard_drop now would commit the current vertical L near center-right, likely creating a tall awkward column; soft_drop also commits downward before alignment. Rotating could help later, but first shifting right better targets the open right-side contour around cols 6-8 where an L can tuck more naturally and set up upcoming T/L/J pieces. Thus move right now.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active T is near the top, centered around columns 4-6. The stack is low and there is no immediate danger, so the main goal is efficient placement for future surface quality. The current board has a useful vertical notch around column 6 above the existing L/O/I structures; rotating the T to a vertical orientation now preserves flexibility and sets up a cleaner tuck/drop path than moving first in its flat spawn shape. Candidate actions: left/right only shift without improving fit yet; soft/hard drop now would commit to a mediocre high placement; no_op wastes time. Rotating right moves the T into the stronger vertical orientation immediately and keeps options open next turn.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a T in rotation 1, high near the top with open space below. The stack is uneven, with a notable central-right cavity and no immediate top-out threat. Moving right shifts the T toward the central-right structures where a T can likely fit better on subsequent drops/rotations than if left or hard-dropped now.

experiences.intentions: Current active piece is a T in rotation 1, sitting high above a mid-right stack. Surface has a notable cavity/indent around columns 5-7 above the existing L/O/I stack, and dropping immediately without adjustment risks creating awkward overhangs. Goal is to place the T to improve surface continuity and preserve future placements from next queue L,J,T,O. Comparing actions: hard_drop now commits too early in current orientation; soft_drop also advances without better alignment; left/right could help later but first rotating to a flatter orientation creates a more useful landing shape. Rotation 2 makes the T flat with the nub downward, a versatile shape for slotting into the central-right indentation on subsequent moves. So best immediate action is rotate_right.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is L near the top in spawn area. Surface below has a central well and uneven stacks, but there is no immediate danger at the top, so we should prioritize steering the L toward a useful placement rather than dropping prematurely. Current canonical orientation is flat with foot on left; moving horizontally now preserves flexibility, while rotating in place is less urgent. The left side has more open access from the spawn and can support tucking the L into future contours. Candidate actions: hard_drop/soft_drop commit too early from high spawn; rotate may help later but not necessary yet; right moves toward taller central clutter. Best sub-goal is to reposition safely toward the more open left side first.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is L near the top center. The stack below is uneven but not threatening yet; there is open space to maneuver. At the current position, rotating right to orientation 1 is valid and tucks the L into a cleaner vertical shape, while current/certain other orientations collide. Goal now is to place the L efficiently without creating overhangs and to keep flexibility for upcoming J,T,O,Z. Candidate moves: left/right just shift before fixing collision issues; soft/hard drop in current shape is premature; rotate_left may not reach the valid orientation immediately. rotate_right moves directly into the valid orientation and improves placement options.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is J near the top with plenty of vertical space below, so no immediate lock threat. Surface around columns 4-6 has a broad plateau and some central unevenness; preserving flexibility for upcoming T,O,Z,S pieces is best. Candidate moves: hard_drop now would commit the current default orientation without setup; soft_drop just advances downward without improving placement yet; lateral moves could position the J for a cleaner tuck, but first rotating to a vertical J creates more placement options and can fit central wells better on subsequent moves. So the best immediate sub-goal is to orient the J vertically before moving/dropping.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.
